text.title: gcp-resource-hierarchy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gcp-resource-hierarchy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Deeply nested folder hierarchies (4+ levels) to reflect every organizational sub-unit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Policy inheritance becomes difficult to trace and audit. Each extra level adds one more layer to check when debugging IAM denials or org-policy violations.

failure-mode.description: No resource labels on projects, VMs, or buckets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Labels are the only mechanism for cost attribution in BigQuery billing exports and Looker Studio dashboards. Unlabeled resources appear as unattributed spend.

failure-mode.description: Mixing dev and prod workloads inside the same project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Projects are the API-enablement and billing boundary. Mixed-environment projects make cost attribution impossible and increase the blast radius of accidental changes.
